title: Originating AFT via PaymentStream AFT
body_text:
Originating AFT via PaymentStream AFT
1	Introduction to Originating AFT via PaymentStream AFT
1.1	Overview of Originating AFT via PaymentStream AFT
The information in this Part is provided for financial institutions that initiate AFT transactions via
PaymentStreamâ¢
AFT.
PaymentStream
AFT is a product on the
PaymentStream
Direct platform that allows financial institutions and their business member Originators to initiate Canadian or US dollar AFT transactions. The transactions must be directed to financial institutions that are domiciled in Canada.
Your financial institution users access
PaymentStream
AFT via the Client Centre, whereas business member users access the application via a direct URL (as provided by your financial institution). Business member users also require a soft or hard token to access
PaymentStream
AFT, but financial institution users donât.
During enrollment, the Originator determines which of the following service types it will use to initiate AFT transactions on
PaymentStream
AFT:
File Upload
â An Originator creates AFT files using in-house or third-party software and then uploads the files to
PaymentStream
AFT. Files uploaded to
PaymentStream
AFT must conform to Central 1âs AFT file specifications. See Central 1âs AFT File Specifications, Form 2726, for details. These specifications are based on CPA Standard 005, Standards for the Exchange of Financial Data on AFT Files, with some modifications.
Data Entry Automatic Release
â An Originator enters AFT transactions on
PaymentStream
AFT and the system releases them automatically.
Data Entry Manual Release
â An Originator enters AFT transactions on
PaymentStream
AFT and then releases the transactions manually.
An AFT file can contain credit and debit transactions for different purposes (e.g., payroll transactions for office and factory staff, each with a different payday). After an AFT file is manually or automatically released in
PaymentStream
AFT, the file is validated. Central 1 forwards the AFT transactions to the other financial institutions to credit or debit their clientsâ accounts.
The rules and guidelines for AFT transactions are applicable regardless of how the transactions are created or how the file is transmitted to Central 1. For detailed information on other aspects of AFT processing, see the Introduction to AFT Part.
The Originator is responsible for complying with all relevant CPA Rules which include, but arenât limited to, obtaining a Payorâs PAD Agreement from each payor, pre-notifying the payor of PADs (if applicable), and obtaining authorization for sporadic PADs.

1.2	Providing PaymentStream AFT Procedures to Business Members
The
PaymentStream
AFT user guides provide procedures for each service type. You can distribute these user guides to your business members. Your financial institution can customize user guides, but you are legally responsible for any changes you make. When Central 1 notifies financial institutions of changes to the user guides, your financial institution must make the same changes to any customized versions of the user guides.
Business member users access
PaymentStream
AFT via a direct URL provided by your financial institution. They also require a soft or a hard token to access
PaymentStream
AFT. Quick-reference sheets for soft and hard token access are available for distribution. Your financial institution enters the unique AFT URL on the quick-reference sheets before distributing these to business members. See Section 1.5, Originating AFT via PaymentStream AFT Document Library, for the complete list of available documents.

1.3	PaymentStream AFT Availability and Hours of Support
PaymentStream
AFT is available at any time except Sundays from 1:00 am to 3:00 am PT. The delivery of AFT transactions is subject to the deadlines published in Section 13.1, AFT File Delivery Deadlines and Record Entry Limits.
Central 1 provides
PaymentStream
AFT support from 4:30 am to 4:30 pm PT, Monday to Friday (excluding statutory holidays). See Section 1.4, Contacts, Websites, and Other Resources.
